overview: PM-SETU (Pradhan Mantri Skills Enhancement and Training for Upskilling) is a centrally sponsored scheme under the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) aimed at enhancing the employability of youth through industry-aligned skill training programs. It focuses on bridging the skill gap by providing training in emerging and high-demand sectors, with an emphasis on placement-linked outcomes. The scheme operates through a network of Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs), Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Kendras (PMKKs), and other accredited training partners across India.
target_beneficiaries: youth; unemployed; school dropouts; women; SC/ST; minorities; persons with disabilities
eligibility: Indian nationals aged between 15 and 45 years, with minimum educational qualification as per the specific job role (ranging from 5th pass to 12th pass or equivalent), belonging to any category including SC/ST/OBC/Minorities/PwD, and meeting the eligibility criteria defined for the specific trade or course under the scheme.
benefits: Free skill training in NSQF-aligned courses, stipend during training (where applicable), placement assistance, post-placement tracking, certification recognized by NCVT or SSC, industry exposure through on-the-job training, and support for self-employment or wage employment.
financial_support: The scheme provides 100% funding for training costs, including trainer remuneration, raw materials, assessment, and certification fees. In certain cases, a stipend is provided to trainees during the training period, especially for women, SC/ST, and persons with disabilities, as per the norms of the specific project or implementing agency.
application_process: 1. Identify the desired skill course and nearest training center (ITI/PMKK/accredited partner). 2. Visit the center or apply online through the State Skill Development Mission portal. 3. Fill out the application form with personal and educational details. 4. Submit required documents for verification. 5. Undergo counseling and pre-assessment if required. 6. Get enrolled in the selected course. 7. Attend training as per schedule. 8. Appear for final assessment. 9. Receive certification and placement support.
deadlines: Rolling basis — admissions are open throughout the year based on batch commencement schedules at training centers.
implementing_agency: Directorate General of Training (DGT), Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE)

overview: The Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) is a flagship initiative by DPIIT under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, launched on 19th April 2021, to provide financial assistance to early-stage startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry, and commercialization. The scheme operates through a network of eligible incubators across India and aims to support 3,600 entrepreneurs via 300 incubators over four years with a total corpus of INR 945 Crore. Startups can apply independently and free of cost, with no authorized consultants promoted by the scheme.
eligibility: A startup, recognized by DPIIT, incorporated not more than 2 years ago at the time of application. The startup must have a business idea to develop a product or a service with a market fit, viable commercialization, and scope of scaling. The startup should be using technology in its core product or service, or business model, or distribution model, or methodology to solve the problem being targeted. Preference would be given to startups creating innovative solutions in sectors such as social impact, waste management, water management, financial inclusion, education, agriculture, food processing, biotechnology, healthcare, energy, mobility, defence, space, railways, oil and gas, textiles, etc. Startup should not have received more than Rs 10 lakh of monetary support under any other Central or State Government scheme. This does not include prize money from competitions and grand challenges, subsidized working space, founder monthly allowance, access to labs, or access to prototyping facility. Shareholding by Indian promoters in the startup should be at least 51% at the time of application to the incubator for the scheme, as per Companies Act, 2013 and SEBI (ICDR) Regulations, 2018. A startup applicant can avail seed support in the form of grant and debt/convertible debentures each once as per the guidelines of the scheme.
benefits: Financial assistance in the form of grant (up to Rs. 20 Lakhs) for validation of Proof of Concept, prototype development, or product trials, and investment (up to Rs. 50 Lakhs) for market entry, commercialization, or scaling up through convertible debentures or debt or debt-linked instruments. The grant is disbursed in milestone-based installments. Incubators provide physical infrastructure, support for testing and validating ideas, mentoring for prototype or product development or commercialization, and developing capacities in finance, human resources, legal compliances, and other functions. They also provide networking with investors and opportunities for showcasing in various national and international events. Startups are not charged any fees by the incubator or its staff for any process of selection, disbursement, incubation, or monitoring. The Seed Fund is disbursed to eligible startups through eligible incubators across India.
financial_support: Seed Fund to an eligible startup by the incubator shall be disbursed as follows: Up to Rs. 20 Lakhs as grant for validation of Proof of Concept, or prototype development, or product trials. The grant shall be disbursed in milestone-based installments. These milestones can be related to development of prototype, product testing, building a product ready for market launch, etc. Up to Rs. 50 Lakhs of investment for market entry, commercialization, or scaling up through convertible debentures or debt or debt-linked instruments. A startup applicant can avail seed support in the form of grant and debt/convertible debentures each once as per the guidelines of the scheme. For startups being supported through convertible debentures, or debt, or debt-linked instruments, funds shall be provided at a rate of interest of not more than prevailing repo rate. The tenure should be fixed at the time of sanctioning the loan by the incubator, which shall be not more than 60 months (5 years). A moratorium of up to 12 months may be provided for the startups. Because of the early stage of the startups, this shall be unsecured and no guarantee from promoter or third-party will be required.
grant_amount_per_entity: Up to Rs. 20 Lakhs as grant for validation of Proof of Concept, or prototype development, or product trials. Up to Rs. 50 Lakhs of investment for market entry, commercialization, or scaling up through convertible debentures or debt or debt-linked instruments
application_process: 1. Register on startupindia.gov.in if not already registered as a DPIIT-recognized startup. 2. Login to the Startup India Seed Fund Portal using your startup credentials. 3. Click on 'Apply Now'. 4. Fill/upload necessary details as per the fields, including entity details, team profile, problem statement, product/service overview, business model, customer profile, market size, quantum of funds needed, projected utilization plan for funds, etc. 5. Select incubator preferences (up to 3) and funding instrument (Grant/Debt/Debenture) under funding details. 6. Check the self-declaration box and click on Submit. 7. Your application is submitted successfully. 8. Track the progress of your application on the Startup India portal dashboard using your login credentials.
deadlines: Year-round ‘Call for Applications’ for Incubators and Startups. Applications can be filled independently by the startups, free of cost. Incubators to complete 100% selection by – 30 June 2026.
implementing_agency: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry, Government of India

overview: Startup India Initiative is a flagship Government of India program launched in 2016 by DPIIT to foster innovation, support entrepreneurs, and drive sustainable economic growth. It provides recognition, tax benefits, easier compliance, IPR fast-tracking, and access to funding schemes like Fund of Funds and Seed Fund Scheme. The initiative aims to build a robust startup ecosystem across India through simplification, handholding, funding support, and industry-academia partnerships.
target_beneficiaries: startups; entrepreneurs; MSME; women entrepreneurs; agri-startups; DeepTech startups
eligibility: An entity must be incorporated or registered in India as a private limited company, partnership firm, or limited liability partnership; be within 10 years of incorporation/registration (up to 20 years for DeepTech startups); have annual turnover not exceeding INR 100 crore (INR 300 crore for DeepTech); work towards innovation, development, or improvement of products/services or have a scalable business model with high potential for employment generation or wealth creation; and not be formed by splitting up or reconstructing an existing business.
benefits: DPIIT recognition enables access to tax benefits including 100% tax exemption on profits for 3 consecutive years under Section 80-IAC, exemption under Section 56(2)(VIIB) for investments above fair market value up to INR 25 crore, self-certification under 9 labour and 3 environment laws, fast-tracking of patent applications with 80% rebate in fees, exemption from Earnest Money Deposit in government tenders, relaxed norms in public procurement, faster winding up within 90 days, access to Fund of Funds and Seed Fund Scheme, credit guarantee support, and participation in initiatives like MAARG mentorship platform, BHASKAR knowledge registry, and National Startup Awards.
financial_support: Financial support is provided through the Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) with a total corpus of INR 10,000 crore, managed by SIDBI, which invests in SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) that in turn fund startups. The Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) provides financial assistance for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry, and commercialisation. The Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups (CGSS) offers credit guarantee up to ₹20 crore per borrower for loans from Member Institutions. Additionally, the Fund of Funds 2.0 supports deep tech, early-stage, manufacturing, and sector/stage agnostic startups through segmented AIFs.
grant_amount_per_entity: Up to INR 20 Lakhs from SISFS for early-stage validation
application_process: 1. Register on the Startup India portal (startupindia.gov.in). 2. Apply for DPIIT recognition via the National Single Window System (NSWS) at nsws.gov.in by adding 'Registration as a Startup' to approvals. 3. Fill the application form with entity details, innovation description, and scalability. 4. Upload required documents: Certificate of Incorporation/Registration, PAN, Memorandum of Association/LLP Deed, Board Resolution (if any), annual accounts and income tax returns for last three financial years. 5. Submit the application and await approval (typically 2-7 business days). 6. Upon recognition, access benefits via Startup India portal and apply for specific schemes like Seed Fund Scheme or tax exemptions through respective portals.
deadlines: Rolling basis — no fixed deadline. Applications are accepted year-round.
implementing_agency: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry

overview: The Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups (CGSS) is aimed at providing credit guarantee up to a specified limit against loans extended by Member Institutions (MIs) to finance eligible borrowers viz. Startups as defined in the Gazette Notification issued by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) and amended from time to time. The Government of India established the Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups with a fixed corpus for providing credit guarantees to loans extended to DPIIT recognised startups by Scheduled Commercial Banks, Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) and Venture Debt Funds (VDFs) under SEBI registered Alternative Investment Funds. The revised framework has enhanced guarantee coverage, increasing the maximum limit from ₹10 crore to ₹20 crore per eligible borrower. CGSS does not provide guarantee cover to DPIIT recognised startups directly, but through a Trustee (NCGTC), which in turn provides guarantee cover to MIs who provide loans to startups.
eligibility: Borrower: The eligibility criteria for an entity to borrow under the Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups shall be as follows, wherein an entity should be: Startup as recognized by DPIIT as per Gazette Notifications issued from time to time, and Startup not in default to any lending/investing institution and not classified as Non-Performing Asset as per RBI guidelines, and Startup whose eligibility is certified by the Member Institution for the purpose of guarantee cover. Lending/Investing Institutions: The eligibility criteria for the lending/investing institutions under the Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups shall be as follows: Scheduled Commercial Banks and Financial Institutions, RBI registered Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) having a rating of BBB and above as rated by external credit rating agencies accredited by RBI and having minimum networth of Rs. 100 crore. However, it may be noted that in case an NBFC subsequently becomes ineligible, due to a downgrade in the credit rating below BBB, the NBFC shall not be eligible for further guarantee cover till upgradation again to eligible category. SEBI registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs).
benefits: The instruments of assistance would be in the form of Venture debt, working capital, subordinated debt/mezzanine debt, debentures, optionally convertible debt and other fund-based as well as non-fund-based facilities, which have crystallised as debt obligations. Credit guarantee coverage under this model would be either transaction-based or umbrella-based. For transaction-based guarantee cover: The Trust shall provide guarantee cover, subject to a maximum of Rs. 20 crore per borrower, as per the details given below: 85% of the amount in default for loan amount up to Rs. 10 crore; 75% of the amount in default for loan amount exceeding Rs. 10 crore. For umbrella-based guarantee cover: The Trust shall provide guarantee cover of actual losses or up to a maximum of 5% of Pooled Investment on which cover is being taken from the fund in Startups, whichever is lower, subject to a maximum of Rs. 20 crore per borrower. Losses are defined as aggregate of principal investments of written off assets along with three months accrued interest from the date of default. In case of partially written off assets, only the principal portion written off along with three months accrued interest thereon from the date of default will be accounted for the loss assets. The umbrella-based guarantee cover will run through the life of the venture debt fund.
financial_support: The maximum amount of debt (fund-based or non-fund-based facilities) eligible for guarantee cover under the Scheme has been revised to ₹20 crore per borrower. The Government of India established the Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups with a fixed corpus for providing credit guarantees to loans extended to DPIIT recognised startups by Scheduled Commercial Banks, Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) and Venture Debt Funds (VDFs) under SEBI registered Alternative Investment Funds. The revised framework has enhanced guarantee coverage, increasing the maximum limit from ₹10 crore to ₹20 crore per eligible borrower.
application_process: 1. Online Application: Visit the Jan Samarth Portal using the link below and complete the online application: Apply on Jan Samarth Portal. For step-by-step guidance, please refer to the Jan Samarth Portal instructional video: Watch Instructional Video. 2. Offline Application: Approach the nearest branch of any Member Lending Institution (MLI) directly and enquire about the required process. The list of eligible MLIs is available in the designated reference link/document: View Eligible MLIs. 3. Registration Process: All eligible institutions may register itself under the said scheme by submitting a signed undertaking (format given on website) and Board Resolution. Upon successful registration of the Member Institution (MI), login credentials of the MI shall be created whereafter it can apply for guarantee cover on NCGTC’s portal. To know more and register as an MI, visit the NCGTC 's portal.

overview: TIDE 2.0 (Technology Incubation and Development of Entrepreneurs 2.0) is a Government of India scheme under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) that promotes tech entrepreneurship through financial and technical support to incubators engaged in supporting ICT startups using emerging technologies such as IoT, AI, Block-chain, Robotics etc. in seven pre-identified areas of societal relevance. The scheme will be implemented through 51 incubators and handholding of approximately 2000 tech start-ups over a period of five years. It aims to create a holistic ecosystem to support technology startups and incubation centers by identifying and creating necessary linkages, and to set up MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) under TIDE 2.0 to ensure synergistic linkages among the TIDE 2.0 centres, theme based incubation centres, Centre of Excellences on emerging technologies and other collaborative platforms.
eligibility: Proposals from 51 TIDE 2.0 Incubation Centres are to be obtained from institutions of higher learning and R&D organizations having incubation facilities and established linkages with large companies, industry bodies, industry clusters so as to provide necessary mentorship and possible infusion of innovative businesses into their larger business operations.
benefits: Financial and technical support to incubators, handholding of tech startups, creation of a networked ecosystem to support technology startups/incubates through identifying and creating linkages, development and management of a comprehensive online platform and ecosystem for promoting virtual incubation across the country, creation of MeitY Startup Hub with a holistic ambition to coordinate the ICT startup-innovation ecosystem in the country, protection of IPR generated through Centre of Excellence in IPR (CoE-IP).
financial_support: The scheme provides grant-in-aid to 51 incubation centres for provisioning of Entrepreneurs-in-residence (EiR) for idea to Proof of Concept up to Rs. 4 Lakh, Grants for prototype development up to Rs. 7 Lakh and Investment for product development and market outreach up to Rs. 40 Lakh. The budgetary outlay of Rs. 264.62 Crores is to be released as grant-in-aid over a period of five years.
grant_amount_per_entity: Entrepreneurs-in-residence (EiR): up to Rs. 4 Lakh; Grants for prototype development: up to Rs. 7 Lakh; Investment for product development and market outreach: up to Rs. 40 Lakh
application_process: 1. Proposals from 51 TIDE 2.0 Incubation Centres are to be obtained from institutions of higher learning and R&D organizations having incubation facilities and established linkages with large companies, industry bodies, industry clusters. 2. The scheme will be implemented through empowering 51 incubators in India and handholding close to 2000 tech start-ups over a period of 5 years. 3. Each of the 51 TIDE 2.0 Centres will be categorized into three distinct groups as summarized below: Group 1 Centers, Group 2 Centers, Group 3 Centers. 4. Release of payment will be in stages as per the budgetary support FY-wise: FY 2018-19: 05.00 Cr, FY 2019-20: 35.94 Cr, FY 2020-21: 52.72 Cr, FY 2021-22: 67.78 Cr, FY 2022-23: 73.13 Cr, FY 2023-24: 30.05 Cr. 5. Stages of release to Incubation Centres: 1st Year: 40% of the amount earmarked for First year on Initiation of the project (MoU to be executed by Implementing Agencies/Partnering Institutes, Terms & Conditions governing release of Grant-in-Aid, Submission of Surety Bond by Implementing Agencies); 6 Months: 60% of the amount earmarked for First year (Submission of Utilization Certificate of Previous release, Technical & Financial Progress Report, Audited statement of accounts, Recommendations of the PRSG); 2nd Year: 50% of the amount earmarked for second year every 6 Months (Submission of Utilization Certificate of Previous release, Technical & Financial Progress Report, Audited statement of accounts, Recommendations of the PRSG); 3rd Year: 50% of the amount earmarked for third year every 6 Months (Submission of Utilization Certificate of Previous release, Technical & Financial Progress Report, Audited statement of accounts, Recommendations of the PRSG); 4th Year: 50% of the amount earmarked for fourth year every 6 Months (Submission of Utilization Certificate of Previous release, Technical & Financial Progress Report, Audited statement of accounts, Recommendations of the PRSG); 5th Year: 50% of the amount earmarked for fifth year every 6 Months (Submission of Utilization Certificate of Previous release, Technical & Financial Progress Report, Audited statement of accounts, Recommendations of the PRSG).
deadlines: The scheme period is of five years from 01 February, 2019 to 31st January, 2024.

overview: NIDHI Prayas is a sub-scheme under the NIDHI Programme of the Department of Science and Technology (DST) that provides financial support and mentorship to innovators for converting their ideas into prototypes. It aims to support early-stage startups and innovators in developing proof-of-concept and prototype development through grant-based assistance. The scheme focuses on promoting innovation and entrepreneurship by offering funding, mentorship, and access to incubation facilities.
target_beneficiaries: innovators; startups; entrepreneurs; students; academic institutions
eligibility: Individual innovators, startups, and student innovators from academic institutions are eligible to apply. The applicant must be an Indian citizen and the innovation must be at the idea or prototype stage. The innovation should have potential for commercialization and social impact. Applicants must be associated with a recognized NIDHI Prayas Centre (incubator) for implementation.
benefits: Financial support for prototype development, access to mentorship and technical guidance, use of fabrication labs and infrastructure at NIDHI Prayas Centres, networking opportunities with investors and industry experts, and support for IP filing and product development.
financial_support: NIDHI Prayas provides grant-in-aid support of up to INR 10 lakhs per innovator or startup for prototype development. The funds are disbursed in milestones based on the progress of the project, subject to review and approval by the NIDHI Prayas Centre. The grant is non-repayable and does not require equity dilution.
application_process: 1. Identify and apply to a recognized NIDHI Prayas Centre (incubator) 2. Submit the application with details of the innovation, team, and development plan 3. Present the idea to the selection committee at the Centre 4. If selected, sign an agreement with the Centre 5. Receive the first tranche of funds upon approval 6. Develop the prototype as per the agreed milestones 7. Submit progress reports and utilization certificates 8. Receive subsequent tranches based on milestone completion 9. Final submission of prototype and project report 10. Receive final payment upon successful completion
deadlines: Rolling basis — applications are accepted throughout the year based on the availability of funds and capacity at NIDHI Prayas Centres.
implementing_agency: Department of Science and Technology (DST), Government of India

overview: The Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) is a Government of India initiative under the Startup India Action Plan, launched in 2016 by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT). It aims to mobilize domestic capital for startups by investing in SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs), which in turn provide equity and equity-linked funding to startups across various sectors and stages. The scheme has a total corpus of INR 10,000 crore and is managed by SIDBI as the implementing agency.
eligibility: The Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) does not invest directly into startups. Instead, it contributes to the corpus of SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs). AIFs must raise the balance 50% or more of the stated fund size from other investors to be eligible for FFS contribution. The FFS shall have representation on the governance structure/board of the venture fund based on the contribution made. Startups seeking indirect benefits must be recognised as 'startups' by the Central Government under DPIIT notification.
benefits: Indirect financial support to startups through SEBI-registered AIFs that receive contributions from FFS. These AIFs invest in startups via equity and equity-linked instruments. The scheme supports a broad mix of sectors including manufacturing, agriculture, health, and education. Up to 5% of returns from FFS are earmarked for ecosystem development activities such as sensitization, workshops, capacity building, plug-and-play shared facilities, mentorship, and regulatory support. The Fund of Funds 2.0 includes a segmented approach targeting deep tech, micro VCs, tech-driven innovative manufacturing, and sector/stage agnostic startups.
financial_support: The Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) operates as a Fund of Funds, meaning it does not invest directly into startups but participates in the capital of SEBI-registered Venture Funds (AIFs). It contributes up to 50% of the stated daughter fund size, provided the AIF has already raised the balance 50% or more from other investors. The total corpus is INR 10,000 crore, disbursed in tranches over the 16th and 17th Finance Commission Cycles. SIDBI acts as the Implementation Agency (IA) for FFS 2.0, continuing its role from FFS 1.0. Funds are disbursed to AIFs based on due diligence and recommendations from a Venture Capital Investment Committee (VCIC) constituted by DPIIT.
application_process: 1. AIFs submit proposals to the Implementation Agency (IA), which includes SIDBI and other selected domestic IAs. 2. IAs conduct due diligence on the proposals. 3. A Venture Capital Investment Committee (VCIC), constituted by DPIIT and comprising industry experts, subject matter experts, and IAs, evaluates the proposals. 4. VCIC considers AIFs managed by experienced professionals with proven track records for funding under the Scheme. 5. Upon approval, AIFs draw funds from FFS in tranches to meet the target corpus. 6. Post-investment, supported AIFs mentor and nurture their investee startups before exit or sale of investments.
deadlines: Commitments to AIFs spread over the 16th and 17th Finance Commission Cycles
implementing_agency: Small Industries Development Bank of India (SIDBI)

overview: Digital India Bhashini is an initiative under the Digital India Programme, implemented by the National e-Governance Division (NeGD) under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India. It aims to facilitate seamless communication between various Indian languages and English, leveraging technology to bridge linguistic divides and promote digital inclusivity. The initiative fosters a unique ecosystem where linguistic diversity finds technological resonance through innovation and collaboration with startups and government agencies.
eligibility: The initiative collaborates with startups and government agencies; specific eligibility criteria for participation are not detailed in the provided evidence.
benefits: Enables seamless multilingual communication, promotes digital inclusivity, supports linguistic diversity through technological innovation, empowers citizens by breaking language barriers, and enhances access to digital services across Indian languages.
financial_support: No specific financial support mechanism, quantum, or type is mentioned in the evidence for beneficiaries or participating entities under Digital India Bhashini.
application_process: No step-by-step application procedure is described in the evidence for accessing or participating in Digital India Bhashini.
deadlines: No application window, submission dates, or deadline information is provided in the evidence.
implementing_agency: National e-Governance Division (NeGD) under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India

overview: Udyam Registration Portal is the official Government of India portal for MSME registration, launched on 1st July 2020 by the Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises. It provides free, paperless, and digital registration for micro, small, and medium enterprises based on self-declaration of investment and turnover. The portal integrates with Income Tax and GST networks for automatic data fetching and issues a dynamic QR-coded certificate with lifetime validity.
target_beneficiaries: MSME; micro enterprises; small enterprises; medium enterprises
eligibility: Any person who intends to establish a micro, small or medium enterprise may file Udyam Registration online based on self-declaration with no requirement to upload documents, papers, certificates or proof. Classification is based on investment in plant and machinery or equipment and turnover: micro enterprise (investment ≤ ₹1 crore and turnover ≤ ₹5 crore), small enterprise (investment ≤ ₹10 crore and turnover ≤ ₹50 crore), medium enterprise (investment ≤ ₹50 crore and turnover ≤ ₹250 crore). All units with GSTIN listed against the same PAN are collectively treated as one enterprise.
benefits: Udyam Registration Certificate with dynamic QR code for verification, no renewal required, access to Priority Sector Lending from banks, eligibility for government subsidies and schemes, facilitation in government procurement via GeM integration, access to credit from financial institutions as per RBI notification, and availing of concessions and rebates under various central and state government schemes.
financial_support: Udyam Registration itself does not provide direct financial support but is mandatory to avail benefits under most Ministry of MSME schemes and to access credit from financial institutions as per RBI notification no. RBI/2020-2021/26 dated 21st August, 2020. The portal enables Priority Sector Lending for registered MSMEs.
application_process: 1. Visit the Udyam Registration Portal at https://udyamregistration.gov.in 2. Click on 'For New Enterprise who are not Registered yet as MSME' 3. Enter Aadhaar number and validate 4. Enter PAN details 5. Fill in enterprise details including name, type, address, bank details 6. Provide investment and turnover details (auto-fetched from IT/GST if available) 7. Submit self-declaration 8. Receive Udyam Registration Certificate with dynamic QR code
deadlines: Rolling basis — no fixed deadline. Applications are accepted year-round.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises

overview: The Annapurna Scheme is a loan product offered by State Bank of India (SBI) aimed at supporting women entrepreneurs in setting up food catering units. It provides financial assistance to women who wish to start or expand their food-related businesses, particularly in the micro-enterprise segment. The scheme is designed to promote self-employment and entrepreneurship among women by offering collateral-free loans up to a certain limit. It is part of SBI's broader initiative to support financial inclusion and women's economic empowerment.
eligibility: The scheme is available to women entrepreneurs who wish to set up food catering units. Applicants must be Indian citizens and meet the bank's creditworthiness criteria. The business should be a proprietary concern owned and managed by a woman. The scheme typically targets micro-enterprises in the food processing or catering sector.
benefits: The scheme provides collateral-free loans up to a specified limit, enabling women to start or expand their food catering businesses. It offers flexible repayment terms and competitive interest rates. The loan can be used for setting up kitchen infrastructure, purchasing equipment, and meeting working capital requirements. It supports women in becoming financially independent and contributes to their socio-economic upliftment.
financial_support: Under the Annapurna Scheme, SBI offers loans up to a maximum of ₹50,000 to women entrepreneurs for setting up food catering units. The loan is provided without requiring collateral or third-party guarantee. The interest rate is linked to the bank's base rate or MCLR, with a concessional spread for women borrowers. Repayment is typically structured over a period of up to 36 months in equated monthly instalments (EMIs).
application_process: 1. Visit the nearest SBI branch or apply through the YONO SBI app or internet banking portal. 2. Fill out the loan application form for the Annapurna Scheme. 3. Submit required documents including identity proof, address proof, business plan, and photographs. 4. The bank will verify the documents and assess the creditworthiness of the applicant. 5. Upon approval, the loan amount will be disbursed to the applicant's account. 6. The applicant can then utilise the funds for setting up the food catering unit as per the business plan.
